code.label: Code & Files
code.description: Read, search, and manage repository code and branches

issues.label: Issues
issues.description: Create, search, and manage GitHub issues

pull_requests.label: Pull Requests
pull_requests.description: Create, review, and merge pull requests

ci.label: CI / Actions
ci.description: Monitor GitHub Actions workflows and debug failures

repo_admin.label: Repository Management
repo_admin.description: Create, delete, fork, and discover repositories

tools.name: get_me
tools.description: Get the authenticated user's profile. Use this to find out who you are authenticated as before performing operations.

tools.name: list_repos
tools.description: List repositories for the authenticated user. Use get_repo for full metadata.

tools.name: get_repo
tools.description: Get details for a repository (description, default branch, visibility, topics, stats).

tools.name: search_repos
tools.description: Search GitHub repositories and return compact repository summaries. Use fullName (owner/repo) with get_repo for full metadata.

tools.name: get_file_contents
tools.description: Get the content of a file from a repository. Returns decoded UTF-8 text. Use ref to read from a specific branch, tag, or commit. Call get_repo_tree first to discover file paths.

tools.name: get_repo_tree
tools.description: Get a compact repository tree summary (path/type/sha) with optional path filtering. Results are capped; use get_file_contents with path+ref for full file content.

tools.name: search_code
tools.description: Search code and return compact matches (repository, path, sha). Use get_file_contents for full file text.

tools.name: list_branches
tools.description: List branches in a repository. Supports pagination and filtering by protection status.

tools.name: list_commits
tools.description: List commits as compact summaries. Filter by branch/tag (sha), file path, or author; use get_commit for full commit details.

tools.name: get_commit
tools.description: Get the full details of a specific commit including its message, author, file changes, and diff stats.

tools.name: list_tags
tools.description: List tags for a repository. Use this to see available versions before creating a release.

tools.name: list_issues
tools.description: List issues as compact summaries (includes PR marker). Filter by state/labels/assignee; use get_issue for full issue details.

tools.name: get_issue
tools.description: Get full details of a specific issue including its body, labels, assignees, and milestone.

tools.name: list_issue_comments
tools.description: List all comments on an issue. Use this to read the full discussion thread before replying.

tools.name: search_issues
tools.description: Search issues and return compact summaries. Use owner/repo + issue number with get_issue for full details.

tools.name: list_labels
tools.description: List all labels available in a repository. Call this before add_labels_to_issue to see which labels exist.

tools.name: list_pull_requests
tools.description: List pull requests as compact summaries. Filter by state/head/base; use get_pull_request for full details.

tools.name: get_pull_request
tools.description: Get full details of a specific pull request including title, body, state, merge status, head/base refs, reviewers, and labels.

tools.name: get_pull_request_diff
tools.description: Get the raw unified diff for a pull request. Returns the complete diff of all changes. For per-file details, use list_pull_request_files instead.

tools.name: list_pull_request_files
tools.description: List the files changed in a pull request with their status (added/modified/deleted) and patch diff per file. Essential for code review.

tools.name: list_pull_request_comments
tools.description: List inline review comments on a pull request (comments attached to specific lines of code).

tools.name: search_pull_requests
tools.description: Search pull requests and return compact summaries. Use owner/repo + pull number with get_pull_request for full details.

tools.name: list_releases
tools.description: List all releases for a repository, newest first.

tools.name: get_latest_release
tools.description: Get the latest published release for a repository. Use this to quickly check the current version.

tools.name: list_workflow_runs
tools.description: List GitHub Actions workflow runs as compact summaries. Use get_workflow_run for full run details.

tools.name: get_workflow_run
tools.description: Get details of a specific GitHub Actions workflow run including its status, conclusion, timing, and associated commit.

tools.name: get_job_logs
tools.description: Get the log output for a specific GitHub Actions workflow job. Use this to diagnose CI failures. Get job_id from the GitHub Actions UI or the jobs list of a workflow run.

tools.name: create_repo
tools.description: Create a new GitHub repository under the authenticated user's account.

tools.name: delete_repo
tools.description: Permanently delete a repository. This is irreversible. Requires the delete_repo scope on classic PATs.

tools.name: fork_repo
tools.description: Fork a repository into your account or an organization. The fork is created asynchronously; the response returns immediately with the new repo details.

tools.name: create_branch
tools.description: Create a new branch in a repository, branching from the repo's default branch by default.

tools.name: delete_branch
tools.description: Delete a branch. Typically used after a pull request is merged. Use list_branches to find the branch name first.

tools.name: edit_file
tools.description: Edit a file using search/replace. Fetches the file, applies edits, and commits the result. Each old_text must match the file content exactly, including whitespace and indentation. Use get_file_contents to verify content before editing. For multi-file atomic changes, use edit_files instead.

tools.name: edit_files
tools.description: Create, edit, and delete multiple files in a single atomic commit. Use action 'create' with content for new files, 'edit' with old_text/new_text search/replace pairs for existing files, 'delete' to remove files. All changes are committed together. For single-file edits, use edit_file instead.

tools.name: create_file
tools.description: Create a new file or overwrite an existing file's content in a single commit. Handles SHA resolution internally -- no need to fetch the file first. For partial edits to existing files, use edit_file instead.

tools.name: delete_file
tools.description: Delete a file from a repository. The file's SHA is fetched automatically. Creates a commit with the deletion.

tools.name: create_pull_request
tools.description: Open a new pull request from a head branch into a base branch.

tools.name: update_pull_request
tools.description: Edit a pull request's title, body, state (open/closed), base branch, or draft status.

tools.name: merge_pull_request
tools.description: Merge a pull request. Supports merge, squash, and rebase merge methods.

tools.name: request_pull_request_reviewers
tools.description: Request specific users or teams to review a pull request.

tools.name: create_pull_request_review
tools.description: Submit a pull request review. Use event=APPROVE to approve, REQUEST_CHANGES to request changes, or COMMENT to leave a comment-only review.

tools.name: create_issue
tools.description: Create a new issue in a repository. Optionally assign users and add labels.

tools.name: update_issue
tools.description: Update fields on an existing issue (title, body, state, assignees, labels, milestone).

tools.name: close_issue
tools.description: Close an issue.

tools.name: comment_on_issue
tools.description: Add a comment to an issue or pull request (GitHub PRs share the issue comment thread). Use list_issue_comments to read existing comments first.

tools.name: add_labels_to_issue
tools.description: Add labels to an issue or pull request. Use list_labels to discover available labels before calling this.

tools.name: create_release
tools.description: Create a new release from a tag. Can auto-generate release notes from commits. Set draft=true to save without publishing, prerelease=true for alpha/beta/rc versions.
